title: GPT-5.6 Leaks Hint at June Launch With Major AI Upgrades
summary: GPT-5.6 Leaks Hint at June Launch With Major AI Upgrades - WinCentral - Windows 11 News - Leaks & Rumors - Windows 10 Tutorials - Windows 10 Mobile Tutorials Search Home Latest News AI News GPT-5.6 Leaks Suggest OpenAI’s Next Big AI Upgrade Could Arrive in June Share OpenAI may already be prep
content: Home Latest News [...] Could Arrive in June [...] OpenAI may already be preparing its next major AI release only weeks after launching GPT-5.5. New leaks, backend testing references, and developer discoveries are fueling speculation that GPT-5.6 is now deep in internal testing and could launch as early as June 2026. Reports also hint at a more powerful “GPT-5.6 Pro” variant focused on advanced reasoning and agentic AI workflows. [...] Developers and AI researchers recently spotted references to GPT-5.6 inside OpenAI-related testing environments and Codex rollout logs. Several internal tags including “iris-alpha,” “ember-alpha,” and “beacon-alpha” were reportedly discovered during development builds, suggesting OpenAI may currently be testing multiple variants of the model. [...] The leak pattern looks familiar. Similar canary rollout references appeared before the public launch of GPT-5.5, which means OpenAI may once again be quietly evaluating the new model using limited internal traffic before announcing it publicly. [...] One of the most interesting details comes from claims that OpenAI researchers are already using the upcoming model internally for debugging and technical workflows. According to multiple reports, the model behind a recent math and [...] Current speculation suggests OpenAI may launch two separate models: [...] 1. GPT-5.6 2. GPT-5.6 Pro [...] The Pro version is expected to target developers, enterprises, and heavy technical users who need stronger reasoning, advanced coding support, and high-end agentic workflows. Meanwhile, the standard GPT-5.6 model could become the mainstream successor to GPT-5.5 inside ChatGPT and OpenAI APIs. [...] The timing is especially interesting because several major AI releases are expected next month. Alongside GPT-5.6, industry rumors also suggest launches for: [...] - Claude Sonnet 4.8 from Anthropic - Gemini 3.5 Pro from Google - Potential new open-source AI model announcements [...] With OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic all preparing major upgrades simultaneously, June 2026 could become one of the biggest months for AI competition yet. [...] GPT-5.5 launched only recently, yet GPT-5.6 references are already appearing in developer environments and backend testing logs. This rapid iteration cycle suggests OpenAI is accelerating development significantly as competition in AI reasoning and coding heats up. [...] If the leaks prove accurate, GPT-5.6 may focus less on flashy benchmark jumps and more on practical real-world intelligence improvements — especially coding reliability, reasoning consistency, autonomous workflows, and frontend generation.

title: Mythos-1 in Claude Code and Claude Security: Anthropic Readies the Most Powerful Cyber Frontier Model of 2026
summary: Mythos-1 in Claude Code and Claude Security: Anthropic Readies the Most Powerful Cyber Frontier Model of 2026 news Mythos-1 in Claude Code and Claude Security: Anthropic Readies the Most Powerful Cyber Frontier Model of 2026 Anthropic readies Mythos-1 for Claude Code and Claude Security:
content: The claude-mythos-1-preview tag has surfaced in the source code of Anthropic's products alongside an explicit string: "Access to the Claude Mythos model in Claude Code and Claude Security". A few days later, a Telegram user briefly intercepted the same model inside their own interface before it was pulled. The sum of these technical breadcrumbs, reported on May 23, 2026 by TestingCatalog under Alexey Shabanov's byline, captures what is now a fact: Anthropic is integrating Mythos-1 (the preview version of the most capable frontier model ever built on the cybersecurity front) into the production pipelines of Claude Code and into the new Claude Security suite, and a general release is now close at hand. [...] - Mythos-1 is being integrated directly into Claude Code (the agentic development assistant) and Claude Security, an Enterprise product with a dedicated dashboard for vulnerability triage. [...] The pattern is consistent with what was reported earlier this month by those who intercepted Claude Mythos [...] Google Cloud's Vertex AI console [...] the Preview tag [...] toward commercial release [...] official launch date [...] 26. [...] - General release is close but not immediate: Anthropic will pair Mythos with new safeguards, expected alongside the next Opus, and in the meantime is distributing the model only to Glasswing partners and Cyber Verification Program researchers. [...] No official date has been published by Anthropic. The strings in the code and the intercepted UI selectors indicate that the technical integration is ready, but release depends on the rollout of the new safeguards, expected with the next Opus. The realistic window flagged by observers is between late June and early July 2026, still for the narrow circle of Glasswing partners and Cyber Verification Program customers. [...] The picture that emerges is that of a lab which has chosen not to switch off its cyber innovation but to confine it inside an operational cage made of vetted partners, structured CVD processes, targeted donations to the open-source ecosystem and a governance framework written in black and white before the model lands on the market. Mythos-1 in Claude Code and Claude Security is not yet a commercial reality, but the traces in the source, the intercepted screenshots, the Glasswing metrics and the public debate of these weeks indicate that the launch window is open. For those who work in security, software development and compliance, the moment to study the new balance is now, not when the model is in their selection .

title: Claude Sonnet 4.8: The 512,000-Line Leak Reveals Anthropic's Next Model
summary: Claude Sonnet 4.8: The 512,000-Line Leak Reveals Anthropic's Next Model news Claude Sonnet 4.8: The 512,000-Line Leak Reveals Anthropic's Next Model A 59.8 MB source map mistakenly published in the Claude Code npm package exposes 512,000 lines and internal references to Sonnet 4.8.
content: , the string [...] sonnet-4-8" [...] a reference to a model that Anthropic [...] that, as [...] As of today, May 24, 2026, Anthropic has not yet officially announced Sonnet 4.8. The Code with Claude conference held in San Francisco on May 6, pointed out by many as the natural stage for the release, passed without an announcement of the new model: the official Anthropic news page at the time of checking does not carry any Sonnet 4.8 release statement, and the current reference for the Sonnet family remains 4.6, released on February 17, 2026. The public flagship continues to be Opus 4.7, already described in our pieces on the comparison with Moonshot's Kimi K2.5 and on the Claude Agent SDK benchmark against LangGraph and CrewAI. [...] Anthropic's track record, however, suggests that the release is now a matter of weeks. Sonnet has always followed Opus by three or four weeks: with Opus 4.7 released on April 16, the arrival of Sonnet 4.8 in May would have been the natural forecast, but the calendar has already slipped into the second half of the month. The most likely explanation is a combination of extended safety testing on the updated tokenizer (the token increase can break fixed-budget applications without warning) and ongoing evaluations on the compatibility of the new`xhigh` tier with Sonnet pricing, historically positioned at 3 and 15 dollars per million tokens. It is plausible to expect an announcement in the second half of June 2026, but this is a forecast, not a confirmation. [...] No. As of May 24, 2026, the model is not available on the Anthropic API, on Amazon Bedrock, or on Google Vertex AI. The most recent public Sonnet family member remains 4.6, and the flagship remains Opus 4.7. The presence of the name "sonnet-4-8" inside the source code is a very strong clue, but it remains technically unofficial until an Anthropic announcement. [...] It remains to be seen whether the hit taken by Anthropic, in terms of exposed intellectual property and eroded competitive advantage, will be offset by the fact that the community has already learned to use the tool better. For developers, the priority now is twofold: update Claude Code to a version later than 2.1.88, and keep an eye on the official Anthropic blog over the coming weeks, because the Sonnet 4.8 announcement, barring surprises, is now a matter of time. For any doubts about the impact of the new tokenizer on an active project, the contact form at the bottom of the page remains the most direct channel for a personalized assessment.

title: Anthropic MCP Tunnels Kill the $670K Shadow AI Tax
summary: Anthropic MCP Tunnels Kill the $670K Shadow AI Tax | THE D[AI]LY BRIEF Anthropic MCP Tunnels Kill the $670K Shadow AI Tax Anthropic just shipped self-hosted sandboxes and MCP tunnels for Claude agents. Here is the decision matrix CIOs need before the next $4.44M AI breach.
content: On May 19, Anthropic quietly shipped the feature that most enterprise AI projects have been blocked on for the past 18 months: a way to run Claude agents against private internal systems without piercing the corporate firewall, exposing internal MCP servers to the public internet, or letting sensitive files leave the customer environment. Self-hosted sandboxes for Claude Managed Agents are now in public beta. MCP tunnels are in research preview. Both target the same enterprise reality CISOs have been screaming about since IBM's 2025 Cost of a Data Breach Report put a number on it: organizations with high shadow AI exposure pay an extra $670,000 per breach, and of breached AI deployments lacked proper access controls in the first place. [...] This is the architectural answer to a problem the model couldn't solve. Claude Opus 4.7 can be the smartest agent on the planet, but if it has to call your private Snowflake instance through a public endpoint, your compliance team will kill the project. Anthropic just removed that excuse. [...] Anthropic's May 19 update to Claude Managed Agents introduces two distinct [...] that, together, let enterprises deploy [...] inside their security perimeter instead of around it [...] tools and data sources to AI applications. In practice, that meant either running MCP servers on public infrastructure (compliance non- [...] for most enterprises) or threading them through reverse proxies, VPNs, and inbound firewall exceptions that security teams refused to grant. [...] 19, Anthropic quietly shipped the feature that most enterprise AI projects have been blocked on for the past 18 months: a way to run Claude agents against private internal systems without piercing the corporate firewall, exposing internal [...] servers to the public internet, or letting sensitive [...] . Self-hosted sandboxes for [...] are now in public beta. [...] are in research preview. [...] On May 19, Anthropic quietly shipped the feature that most enterprise AI projects have been blocked on for the past 18 months: a way to run Claude agents against private internal systems without piercing the corporate firewall, exposing internal MCP servers to the public internet, or letting sensitive files leave the customer environment. Self-hosted sandboxes for Claude Managed Agents are now in public beta. MCP tunnels are in research preview. title: Anthropic warns Claude Mythos Preview finds bugs faster than developers can patch them
summary: Anthropic warns Claude Mythos Preview finds bugs faster than developers can patch them Anthropic warns Claude Mythos Preview finds bugs faster than developers can patch them May 23, 2026 Anthropic Key Points - Anthropic and around 50 partners used the Claude Mythos Preview AI model to iden
content: Anthropic warns Claude Mythos Preview finds bugs faster than developers can patch them [...] - Anthropic and around 50 partners used the Claude Mythos Preview AI model to identify more than 10,000 critical security vulnerabilities in system-relevant software within just one month. - The rate of discovery already outpaces the capacity to verify and fix the identified flaws, according to the company. - Anthropic warns of a dangerous transition period, as AI models like Claude Mythos can detect vulnerabilities far faster than organizations can patch them, creating a growing security gap. [...] A month after launching Project Glasswing, Anthropic is sharing its first results. The company says its Claude Mythos Preview AI model, working with about 50 partners, has found more than 10,000 high- or critical-severity vulnerabilities in system-critical software. [...] The model now spots security flaws faster than teams can verify, disclose, and patch them, Anthropic writes in a blog post. The company is holding back specific technical details, since the standard industry deadline for disclosing new vulnerabilities is 90 days and most findings can't be described yet without putting end users at risk. [...] Outside reviews back up these numbers. The UK's AI Security Institute says the latest Mythos Preview checkpoint is the first model to fully solve both of its in-house cyber ranges—simulated multi-stage cyberattacks. Independent security platform XBOW calls the model a major step beyond all prior models, citing "unprecedented precision." Anthropic says Mythos Preview also tops the academic benchmarks ExploitBench and ExploitGym, with GPT-5.5 being close in most of these benchmarks and already openly available. [...] Alongside partner work, Anthropic says it scanned more than 1,000 open-source projects with Mythos Preview. The model estimates it found 6,202 high- or critical-severity vulnerabilities, with 23,019 total findings across all severity levels. [...] critical. Based on those [...] close to 3 [...] cybersecurity skills will soon be [...] GPT-5.5 fits the profile, and there's also a more specialized variant called GPT-5.5 Cyber, though it's unclear what exactly sets the two apart. [...] Either way, these new capabilities create a transition period where vulnerabilities get found fast but patched slowly. That gap brings new risks, Anthropic says. Mythos-class models slash the time and cost of finding and exploiting flaws. No company, Anthropic included, has built safeguards strong enough to stop misuse of these models and prevent serious damage.

title: Anthropic Launches Claude For Small Business With 10-City SMB Tour
summary: Anthropic Launches Claude For Small Business With 10-City SMB Tour - Self Employed Anthropic launched Claude for Small Business on May 13, 2026, a packaged set of connectors and 15 ready-to-run agentic workflows that put Claude inside the tools small businesses already run.
content: Anthropic Launches Claude For Small Business With 10-City SMB Tour - Self Employed [...] Anthropic launched Claude for Small Business on May 13, 2026, a packaged set of connectors and 15 ready-to-run agentic workflows that put Claude inside the tools small businesses already run. The launch includes connectors for Intuit QuickBooks, PayPal, HubSpot, Canva, DocuSign, Google Workspace, and Microsoft 365, plus a 10-city free training tour that begins May 14 in Chicago. [...] For self- [...] , the launch matters because the named [...] the late- [...] -season prep, contract review, and lead triage are now templated jobs that Claude can run within [...] owner’s existing stack, with approval gates before anything is sent, posted, or paid. [...] The package is a toggle-install within Claude Cowork that activates 15 workflows across finance, operations, sales, marketing, HR, and customer service. Named workflows include payroll planning that settles QuickBooks cash against incoming PayPal settlements, month-end close that reconciles books and writes a plain-English profit and loss statement, and a campaign builder that pulls HubSpot performance and generates assets in Canva. [...] Anthropic also bundled 1 [...] based on the [...] as the most [...] invoice chaser [...] month-end [...] tax-season organizer, [...] contract reviewer, [...] content strategist. [...] The connector list is what sets this launch apart for solo and micro-businesses. QuickBooks, PayPal, HubSpot, Canva, DocuSign, Google Workspace, and Microsoft 365 are the same stack that millions of self-employed owners already pay for, so adoption does not require ripping out tools or learning a new accounting system. [...] The workflow framing is also new. Most prior AI tooling for solopreneurs stopped at the chat window, leaving the owner to copy and paste between systems. The agentic workflow approach moves that work into Claude, reads from the connected tool, and queues actions such as reminder emails or month-end packets for the owner to approve. [...] Owners who already pay for Claude on a Team or Enterprise plan can toggle Claude for [...] Business inside Claude Cowork and connect the tools they already use. The setup expects the same logins the owner uses every day, so the lift is closer to authorizing a Zapier integration than installing new software. [...] Owners in Chicago, Tulsa, Dallas, Hamilton Township, Baton Rouge, Birmingham, Salt Lake City, Baltimore, San Jose, or Indianapolis can register for a free half-day SMB Tour workshop. Attendees get hands-on training plus a one-month Claude Max subscription to test the workflows on their own books.
